name: Encapsulated rapamycin lifespan study in aged male C57BL/6J mice
preprompt: You are refining a lifespan study protocol for aged male C57BL/6J mice. Keep the question focused on the primary survival endpoint, define the comparison clearly (encapsulated rapamycin 14 ppm in chow vs eudragit-only control), and preserve the treatment start age of 12 months. Produce a practical protocol template with executable steps, critical controls, and analysis-ready endpoints.
tooling: Read: own data, TOCs and summaries of all other components, full details on request via orchestrator. Write: own data only.
summary: We are testing whether starting encapsulated rapamycin at 12 months of age increases median lifespan in male C57BL/6J mice relative to eudragit-only control chow. The protocol template should define a clean randomized, blinded survival study with the primary endpoint of median lifespan and minimal confounding from husbandry or diet variability.
